pattern: Scanning right to left while keeping a monotonic stack lets you pin down the largest possible 'middle' value whose 'right' partner has already been seen, so a later smaller element closes the pattern.
hints: You are looking for three positions i < j < k whose values form a low-high-medium shape; fixing which of the three you scan for first changes how hard the search is.
Walk the array from the right and remember, as a running threshold, the best 'medium' value (the k in the pattern) that you can still pair with some larger j to its left.

Keep a decreasing stack of candidate 'high' values; when you pop elements smaller than the current value they become the medium threshold, and any later element below that threshold completes the pattern.

edge_inputs.description: single element cannot form any subsequence of length three
pattern values are present but order forbids it (the small value comes last)
all equal values can never satisfy the strict inequalities
the qualifying low element sits after the high-medium pair

stress.description: random values across a wide range so both true and false outcomes appear
stress.note: wide value spread makes 132 patterns common but not guaranteed

stress.description: small alphabet of values to force many ties and near-patterns
stress.note: tight value range stresses the strict-inequality handling
